channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Tools & Frameworks
channel.description: Latest Tools & Frameworks news in Context Engineering

item.title: Leveraging Computerized Adaptive Testing for Cost-effective Evaluation of Large Language Models in Medical Benchmarking
item.description: arXiv:2603.23506v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid proliferation of large language models (LLMs) in healthcare creates an urgent need for scalable and psychometrically sound evaluation methods. Conventional static benchmarks are costly to administer repeatedly, vulnerable to data contaminati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, LLM, framework, tool, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Internal Safety Collapse in Frontier Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.23509v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work identifies a critical failure mode in frontier large language models (LLMs), which we term Internal Safety Collapse (ISC): under certain task conditions, models enter a state in which they continuously generate harmful content while executin...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, framework, tool, model, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Berta: an open-source, modular tool for AI-enabled clinical documentation
item.description: arXiv:2603.23513v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Commercial AI scribes cost \$99-600 per physician per month, operate as opaque systems, and do not return data to institutional infrastructure, limiting organizational control over data governance, quality improvement, and clinical workflows. We devel...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> audio, platform, tool, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DepthCharge: A Domain-Agnostic Framework for Measuring Depth-Dependent Knowledge in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.23514v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models appear competent when answering general questions but often fail when pushed into domain-specific details. No existing methodology provides an out-of-the-box solution for measuring how deeply LLMs can sustain accurate responses u...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, LLM, framework, analysis, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Environment Maps: Structured Environmental Representations for Long-Horizon Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.23610v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although large language models (LLMs) have advanced rapidly, robust automation of complex software workflows remains an open problem. In long-horizon settings, agents frequently suffer from cascading errors and environmental stochasticity; a single mi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, paper, LLM, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LLMs Do Not Grade Essays Like Humans
item.description: arXiv:2603.23714v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models have recently been proposed as tools for automated essay scoring, but their agreement with human grading remains unclear. In this work, we evaluate how LLM-generated scores compare with human grades and analyze the grading behavi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, tool, model, GPT, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.
item.description: A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt engineering, prompt, platform, paper, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: fastapi_mcp - Expose your FastAPI endpoints as Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, with Auth!
item.description: Expose your FastAPI endpoints as Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, with Auth!<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, context, model, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: parlant - The conversational control layer for customer-facing AI agents - Parlant is a context-engineering framework optimized for controlling customer interactions.
item.description: The conversational control layer for customer-facing AI agents - Parlant is a context-engineering framework optimized for controlling customer interactions.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Efficient Benchmarking of AI Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.23749v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating AI agents on comprehensive benchmarks is expensive because each evaluation requires interactive rollouts with tool use and multi-step reasoning. We study whether small task subsets can preserve agent rankings at substantially lower cost. Un...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, framework, tool, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A New Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents (EVA)
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (Hugging Face Blog)
